Off Calendar Feb-15-2017 Continued To Mar-29-17 At 03:00 P.M. In Dept 504
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John E. Stefanki, counsel for the Grace Plaintiffs; Scott D. Righthand and Brittany Rogers, counsel for Plaintiffs; Mark W. Swanson, counsel for the Critser Plaintiffs; Maria M. Lampasona, counsel for Kysol Industrial; John Rosenthal, counsel for Simplo Technology; James C. Hyde, counsel for Foretravel, Inc. (courtcall); Felicia Jafferies, counsel for Elixir Industries; Gregory Read, counsel for Hewlett-Packard Co., Best Buy Co. Inc., TigerDirect, Inc. and Systemax, Inc.

The Court addresses discovery issues:

Depositions of Michelle Critser: Defense is requesting 3 more hours of deposition time, Plaintiff contends 1 hour should be sufficient. Defense notes objections to questions at the deposition which the deponent was instructed not to answer, noteably, whether the deponent is currently involved in a relationship. After discussion, the Court determines that it will allow 3 hours for additional deposition time for the defendants. counsel are to meet and confer on those lines of questioning to which plaintiffs' counse objected. Mr. Righthand is to letter brief the issue of the current relationship of the deponent but the Court's tentative ruling pending briefing is to allow that line of questioning.

Other depositions: Counsel note that a number of depositions have already concluded but estimate that, including expert witnesses, there are approximately 20 more depositions to take. Depositions, not including expert witnesses, should be completed by May or June 2017.

Counsel advise the Court that there is currently a plan under discussion to relocate the remnants of the RV to the Sacramento area from far northern California. Discussion ensues as to how the cost of storage should be borne. Counsel are to meet and confer once they have completed discussions with their respective clients.

Court and counsel discuss the pending five-year statute, currently 02/01/18, with respect to scheduling trial. Defense counsel are to review the possibility of waiving the statute with their clients then meet and confer with Plaintiffs counsel regarding the same. The Court will leave the current trial date of 11/13/17 in place.

This case management conference is continued to 03/29/17 at 3:00 p.m., a CMC statement to be filed one week before. The Judicial Council form need not be used as counsel should specifically address the issues raised at this conference.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: R.Michael Diles; Bailiff: None. Not reported.

Defendant Samsung Sdi Company'S Motion To Dismiss In Accordance With California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7 And Request For Attorneys Fees And Costs And Monetary Sanctions Pursuant To California Rule Of Court 2.30
Off Calendar May-16-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nicalos Martin of Wilson Elser telephone number 415-433-0990 for Cross-Plaintiff Simplo Technology Logistic Company, LTD.
Carrie Lin of Manion Gaynor & Manning LLP telephone number 415-527-2802 for Cross-Defendant Samsung SDI Co., LTD.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Samsung SDI Company's Motion To Dismiss In Accordance With California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7 And Request For Attorneys Fees And Costs And Monetary Sanctions Pursuant To California Rule Of Court 2.30 is denied without prejudice. The instant record fails to show that Simplo is violating CCP 128.7(b). While the record indicates that Samsung's batteries were not involved in the fire, the limited discovery and inconsistent positions of Hewlett Packard Co. have not entirely foreclosed such a theory of recovery. The court sustains Simplo's objection to the Supplemental Declaration of Carrie Lin and exhibits attached thereto. The document is untimely and the court did not consider it in connection with these motions. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Joinder And Joinder In Defendant Simplo Technology Usa Logistic Company, Ltds Motion To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar Mar-04-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SCOTT RIGHTHAND & ATTY. BRITTANY ROGERS OF THE LAW OFFICE OF SCOTT RIGHTHAND, TEL. NO. 544-0115 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RONALD S. BUSHNER OF THE WILSON ELSER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 625-9380 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT SIMPLO TECHNOLOGY USA;
ATTY. RYAN GREENSPAN OF THE LECLAIR RYAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 913-4965 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT ELIXIR;
ATTY. BRENT M. KARREN OF THE MGM LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 512-438 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT SAMSUNG SDI;
ATTY. JAMES C. HYDE OF THE ROPERS MAJESKI LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (408)287-6262 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT FORETRAVEL INC. BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. BRIAN P. DOLIN OF THE JACOBSEN & MCELROY LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (916)971-4100 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT HEWLETT PACKARD COMPANY BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FORETRAVEL INC.'S Joinder In Defendant Simplo Technology USA Logistic Company, Ltd's Motion To Transfer Venue is Denied. CCP 397(c) provides discretionary power to the court to transfer a case "when the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice would be promoted by the change." The court should only consider the convenience of nonparty witnesses. (Wrin v. Ohlandt (1931) 213 Cal.158, 160.) The declarations from the nonparty witnesses must "set forth the names of the witnesses, the nature of the testimony expected from each and the reasons why attendance upon the trial would be inconvenient." (Edwards v. Pierson (1957) 156 Cal.App.2d 72, 75.) The declarations submitted by Defendant do not set forth "the nature of the testimony expected from each." (Id.) Rather, each of the five declarants state in a conclusory fashion that they responded to the incident and prepared a report of their findings. (Declaration of Nicolas Martin, Exs. I-N.) There is no information provided as to what these declarants will testify about and therefore, the Court cannot determine whether their testimony will be necessary. (See Edwards v. Pierson (1957) 156 Cal.App.2d 72, 75, "the object of the stated requirement of the affidavit is to enable the court to determine the materiality of the evidence or the necessity of testimony from such witnesses.") Because Defendant did not meet its evidentiary burden, the motion is denied. The Court reminds Plaintiffs to use exhibit tabs in any future motions, as required by CRC 3.1110(f). Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 =302/ehg

ATTY. JAMES C. HYDE OF THE ROPERS MAJESKI LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (408)287-6262 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT FORETRAVEL INC. BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. BRIAN P. DOLIN OF THE JACOBSEN & MCELROY LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (916)971-4100 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT HEWLETT PACKARD COMPANY BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SIMPLO TECHNOLOGY USA LOGISTIC COMPANY, LTD'S Motion To Transfer Venue is Denied. CCP 397(c) provides discretionary power to the court to transfer a case "when the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice would be promoted by the change." The court should only consider the convenience of nonparty witnesses. (Wrin v. Ohlandt (1931) 213 Cal.158, 160.) The declarations from the nonparty witnesses must "set forth the names of the witnesses, the nature of the testimony expected from each and the reasons why attendance upon the trial would be inconvenient." (Edwards v. Pierson (1957) 156 Cal.App.2d 72, 75.) The declarations submitted by Defendant do not set forth "the nature of the testimony expected from each." (Id.) Rather, each of the five declarants state in a conclusory fashion that they responded to the incident and prepared a report of their findings. (Declaration of Nicolas Martin, Exs. I-N.) There is no information provided as to what these declarants will testify about and therefore, the Court cannot determine whether their testimony will be necessary. (See Edwards v. Pierson (1957) 156 Cal.App.2d 72, 75, "the object of the stated requirement of the affidavit is to enable the court to determine the materiality of the evidence or the necessity of testimony from such witnesses.") Because Defendant did not meet its evidentiary burden, the motion is denied. The Court reminds Plaintiffs to use exhibit tabs in any future motions, as required by CRC 3.1110(f). Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 =302/ehg
